title: HTTP Caching, Freshness, Validators, Vary, and Invalidation · Browser and Node Client Systems · Spiral Review
summary: A design-system maintainer reconstructing an offline failure handoff must reconstruct the governing policy from partial evidence after delay and distinguish current rules from tempting earlier ones for http caching, freshness, validators, vary, and invalidation, producing spec-aware cache decision notebook. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch spec-aware cache decision notebook after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, e…
objectives: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Compute freshness from explicit directives, dates, age, and response context rather than wall-clock intuition. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Use ETag and Last-Modified validators with If-None-Match or If-Modified-Since and interpret 304 correctly. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Fetch and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Private Shared Cache
steps.instruction:
Order the Private Shared Cache Fetch and TypeScript HTTP evidence cycle for this review task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Fetch and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Private Shared Cache.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Private Shared Cache assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Fetch and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Private Shared Cache, keep Private Shared Cache connected to executable HTTP client decision evidence.

content.text: offline design-system client stores a response with max-age=30, ETag "rev-3", Age:3, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Private Shared Cache has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: For Fetch and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Private Shared Cache, name the method, authority, body, byte, time, and credential boundary, Private Shared Cache precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Fetch and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Private Shared Cache, trace this concrete input and predict resolution, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup evidence before running it: offline design-system client stores a response with max-age=30, ETag "rev-3", Age:3, and Vary: Accept-Language. A user-specific request arrives stale; compute reuse, revalidation, 304 metadata merge, cache key, and privacy policy. Competency probe: Distinguish private, shared, gateway, browser, and application caches plus who may reuse stored responses. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

For Fetch and TypeScript Retrieve the complete evidence cycle · Review Private Shared Cache, challenge the documented misconception with request, response, fault, policy, and transfer evidence: Cache-Control private prevents every form of local response storage. This remains false for TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch.

options.text: Declare Fetch and TypeScript method semantics, target authority, credentials, body ownership, and byte and time budgets for the review Private Shared Cache case
Predict Fetch and TypeScript resolution, transport, redirect, status, representation, retry, and cleanup outcomes for the review Private Shared Cache case
Run the smallest deterministic Fetch and TypeScript request or response model and retain inspectable evidence for the review Private Shared Cache case
Inject one changed Fetch and TypeScript failure, verify bounded cleanup, and state live-network transfer limits for the review Private Shared Cache case
